title: Turkish Breakfast (Kahvalti)
note: Recovery meal after airport transfers.

title: Sunset Cruise + Galata Bridge Walk
note: Best photo window is golden hour from the ferry deck.

title: Grand Bazaar + Spice Bazaar
note: Shopping, snacks, and local products in Eminonu area.

title: Galata / Istiklal / Taksim Walk
note: Use Tunel funicular to skip the steep uphill section.

title: Banthat Thong Core Food Crawl
note: Start with savory first, then dessert/drink stops; check queues before committing.

title: Here Duan (Thai Main Dishes)
note: Reddit pick for strong Thai mains; good first savory stop before sweets.

title: Black Me O Boat Noodles + Khanom Thuai
note: Budget-friendly bowl stop (about 30 THB range reported) plus coconut pudding dessert add-on.

title: Yolk (Egg Custard Cake)
note: Popular dessert stop; queues can move faster later in the evening.

title: June Pang Thick Toast
note: Very filling portions; split one order to avoid sugar overload too early.

title: Nueng Nom Nua Milk Drinks
note: Known for milk bottles and sweet drink pairings after savory stops.

title: Soi Chula Side-Lane Walk
note: Quick exploration of nearby lanes for smaller local spots and lower-key atmosphere.

title: Khao San Road Bar Strip
note: Keep it as a walk-and-observe block with one drink stop rather than a full sit-down night.

title: Patpong Night Market Pass-through
note: Short final neon/market pass-through near your hotel corridor.

title: ICONSIAM Special Stops
note: SookSiam, Haus Nowhere (Sunshine), Apple terrace, ICONCRAFT and river promenade.

title: Song Wat Creative Walk
note: Design cafes, restored shophouses, galleries, and old signage photography.

title: Yaowarat Dinner + Soi Nana Bars
note: Street seafood and crispy pork first, then optional speakeasy cocktails in Chinatown Soi Nana.

title: TripGuru 11-Hour Loop Tour
note: Maeklong Railway, Damnoen Saduak floating market, and Ayutthaya ruins in one go.

title: Mahanakhon Skywalk (Night)
note: Go after a hotel shower. Bangkok's highest observation deck, glass floor, night city views.

title: Jim Thompson House
note: Traditional teak architecture and the history of Thai silk.

title: Siam Mega-Malls
note: Siam Paragon, Discovery, and CentralWorld. Great for A/C lunch.

title: Wat Ratchanatdaram (Loha Prasat)
note: The "Metal Castle" with 37 striking bronze/black spires. Right next to the Golden Mount.

title: Rajadamnern Stadium (Muay Thai)
note: Authentic Thai boxing in the oldest, most prestigious stadium in the country.

title: Thip Samai Pad Thai
note: The legendary Pad Thai wrapped in egg, right next to Jay Fai.

title: Jay Fai Spotting
note: Walk by to see the Michelin-starred chef cooking in her signature ski goggles.

title: Fern Forest Cafe
note: Lush, tropical cafe in the Old City. Perfect for a relaxing post-flight brunch.

title: Twenty Mar
note: Sophisticated, dark aesthetic specialty coffee. 'Adults Only' - great for a quiet 'Dirty Coffee'.

title: Wat Chedi Luang & Wat Phra Singh
note: The massive ruined stupa and the golden heart of the Old City.

title: Huen Phen
note: Dinner. Eccentric dining room packed with Lanna antiques. Excellent Northern Thai food.

title: North Gate Jazz Co-Op
note: Legendary, unpretentious live jazz. Grab a drink and join the crowd spilling out onto the street.

title: Doi Inthanon & Elephants Tour
note: Highest peak in Thailand, waterfalls, and ethical elephant feeding/bathing.

title: Khao Soi Mae Sai
note: Michelin Bib Gourmand Khao Soi. Bustling local canteen vibe.

title: Chiang Mai Night Bazaar
note: Massive daily night market. Check out 'Ploen Ruedee Night Market' inside for food and live music.

title: Zoe in Yellow
note: The absolute epicenter of backpacker and expat partying in the Old City.

title: Freedom Beach via Longtail Boat
note: Hire a longtail boat from Patong Beach to reach this pristine, roadless cove. Go mid-afternoon to avoid crowds.

title: Banzaan Fresh Market
note: Buy fresh raw seafood downstairs and have it cooked to order upstairs in the food court.

title: Bangla Road Nightlife
note: The wildest street in Phuket. Neon lights, open-air beer bars, huge nightclubs, and a crazy party atmosphere.

title: Phang Nga Bay 7 Wonders Tour
note: Full-day speedboat tour. Includes sea canoeing, Koh Panyee floating village, and James Bond Island.

title: Post-Boat Recovery Dinner
note: Keep it easy. Patong beachfront restaurants or a quick return to Banzaan Fresh Market.

title: Aloe Vera or Foot Massage
note: Highly recommended to soothe sunburns and relax after a long day on the water.
